items.text: Verify by Test that the Local Transport Server operates within 150MB resident memory and 5% single-core average CPU utilisation when serving 6 concurrent desklet connections at 10Hz context update rate.
items.rationale: Steady-state RSS ≤150MB and CPU ≤5% under 6 concurrent desklet connections at 10Hz are measurable with process monitoring tools during a sustained load test.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Local Transport Server continues accepting connections and routing heartbeat pings at no less than 1Hz per client during Degraded mode with stale-context notices.
items.rationale: Connection acceptance and heartbeat delivery during Degraded mode are testable by inducing a context update failure and verifying continued connections and 1Hz pings.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Local Transport Server HTTP Static Asset Server serves the desklet web bundle over HTTPS with correct content-hash cache-control headers on the WebSocket Gateway port.
items.rationale: Static asset serving over HTTPS with content-hash caching headers is testable by requesting the desklet web surface bundle and inspecting response headers and TLS configuration.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Channel Multiplexer forwards each inbound desklet intent message to the Intent Router as a JSON envelope containing desklet ID, role ID, and intent payload.
items.rationale: Intent message forwarding completeness and desklet ID embedding are testable by simulating inbound intent messages and inspecting the envelope at the Intent Router.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Channel Multiplexer accepts role-scoped context delta frames from the Workspace Context Store with sequence numbers and delivers them to desklet connections without reordering.
items.rationale: Context delta frame acceptance and sequence number handling are testable by injecting frames and verifying ordering and delivery to the Workspace Context Store.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Heartbeat Monitor reads the full connection list and last-pong timestamps from the Connection Registry within 2ms and can mark connections stale.
items.rationale: Read latency and mark-as-stale ability are testable by querying the Connection Registry under load and measuring response time.

items.text: Verify by Test that the WebSocket Gateway updates the Connection Registry within 10ms of socket connect or disconnect with desklet ID, socket handle, role ID, and timestamp fields.
items.rationale: Registry update latency and field completeness after connect and disconnect events are measurable with instrumented WebSocket sessions.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Local Transport Server exposes HTTP GET /health on the loopback interface and that the Host Core Runtime polls it at 2-second intervals.
items.rationale: Heartbeat endpoint availability and 2-second polling are testable by monitoring HTTP GET /health call frequency and response codes from the loopback interface.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Local Transport Server–Desklet Client Framework interface uses WSS over TLS 1.3 and validates a server-issued session token in the initial HTTP Upgrade handshake.
items.rationale: WSS TLS 1.3 negotiation and session token validation are testable by connecting a desklet client and inspecting TLS parameters and token exchange.

items.text: Verify by Test that IFC-XPT-038: Send HTTP GET / and HTTP GET /static/app.js to the Local Transport Server, and send Connection: Upgrade WebSocket request to the same port. Confirm: GET / returns 200 with HTML body within 300 ms; GET /static/app.js returns 200 with JS body and content-hash ETag; WebSocket upgrade is handled by WebSocket Gateway. Pass: all three requests routed correctly. Fail: any request returns wrong response or wrong handler.
items.rationale: Tests the HTTP upgrade routing interface between HTTP Static Asset Server and WebSocket Gateway on a single port (IFC-XPT-038).

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-XPT-044: Inject LOCK event while 8 active desklet connections are streaming context. Confirm all 8 sockets receive close frame and disconnect within 1 s. Attempt 3 new WebSocket upgrades post-lock; confirm all are rejected. Pass: all 8 closed within 1 s, 3 upgrades rejected. Fail: any socket remains open after 1 s.
items.rationale: Tests the lock safe-state transition from SYS-REQ-003. The post-lock upgrade rejection test ensures the enforcement is persistent, not just a momentary flush.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-XPT-042: With 16 active desklet connections spanning all 6 roles, publish one context update per second for 60 s. At second 30, establish 4 additional connections. Measure jitter of context delivery across all connections throughout. Pass: all connections receive all updates; jitter < 20 ms at all 20 connections. Fail: any update missed or jitter exceeds 20 ms.
items.rationale: Tests the 5 ms latency constraint and role-scoped delivery correctness under maximum fan-out load. Wrong-role delivery is a confidentiality failure (SYS-REQ-011).

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-XPT-041: With 16 active desklet WebSocket connections, invoke a high-CPU intent (e.g. large RAG retrieval) that sustains 70% CPU for 5 s. Continuously measure WebSocket heartbeat latency during the event. Pass: heartbeat latency stays below 100 ms throughout. Fail: heartbeat latency exceeds 100 ms during high-CPU period.
items.rationale: Tests the 5 s detection window from SYS-REQ-008. The 7 s total window accounts for the worst-case poll alignment. Double-fire check ensures idempotency in the event path.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-XPT-040: Start 50 WebSocket handshake processes simultaneously on a single port. Measure time from first SYN to last handshake complete. Pass: all 50 complete within 500 ms; no handshake errors. Fail: any handshake fails or total time exceeds 500 ms.
items.rationale: Directly tests the authentication gate on the WebSocket Gateway. The replay case validates single-use enforcement, which is the primary attack vector on a local LAN.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-XPT-039: Open WebSocket connection without presenting a valid pairing token. Attempt to subscribe to a context stream. Pass: connection rejected at handshake with status 401, no context data sent. Fail: connection accepted or any context data sent before rejection.
items.rationale: Tests the TLS-over-WebSocket binding requirement under maximum expected concurrent desklet load (16 desklets per SYS-REQ-016).

items.text: Verify by Inspection that the Security and Lock Manager code paths for context payload serialisation and pairing responses contain no credential, authentication token, or executable capability fields.
items.rationale: No credentials or capability tokens in outbound payloads is verifiable by inspection of the context serialisation and pairing response code paths.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Host Authenticator detects an OS session-lock event and signals the Lock State Controller within 200ms of the OS event timestamp.
items.rationale: OS session-lock detection latency and signalling to Lock State Controller are testable by triggering an OS lock event and measuring the signal delivery time.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Pairing Token Authority rejects join messages bearing unrecognised, already-redeemed, or expired tokens with no retry permitted, and logs each rejection with desklet fingerprint.
items.rationale: Token rejection for unrecognised, already-redeemed, and expired tokens with rejection logging is testable by submitting invalid tokens and inspecting the log.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-006: After 6 months of simulated clock time, attempt 100 post-expiry workspace restore operations. Pass criterion: all 100 return SNAPSHOT_EXPIRED status with zero workspace state restored; no silent failures.
items.rationale: The synchronous notification requirement (IFC-SLM-007) is most meaningfully tested by checking for the absence of an accepted-post-lock intent, which is the exact harm the requirement prevents. Trace instrumentation allows sub-tick inspection without modifying production code paths.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-005: With 500 snapshots in the Snapshot Store, run a retention sweep. Count snapshots deleted. Pass criterion: all snapshots older than 6 months are deleted; snapshots within the 6-month window are retained.
items.rationale: The 100ms inter-event latency requirement is tested with 20 samples to confirm it is structural (subscription-based) rather than accidental (single cold-start fast path). The 95th percentile criterion allows for rare OS scheduler pre-emption.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-004: Write 200 sequential workspace snapshots to the Snapshot Store and read each back. Pass criterion: every snapshot restored byte-for-byte matches what was saved; no data loss or corruption on any of the 200 cycles.
items.rationale: The 80% CPU load condition replicates worst-case host conditions; verifying all 50 calls (not just the mean) ensures no tail-latency spike that would delay the desklet join within SYS-REQ-006's 3s window.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-003: Restore 100 workspaces from snapshot. Measure restore latency from request to workspace-ready signal. Pass criterion: 95th-percentile restore latency <= 3 s under nominal load.
items.rationale: This test exercises both the role-scoped filtering (SUB-SLM-005) and credential isolation (SUB-SLM-006) in a single stream event, confirming the Context Access Guard correctly intersects its two filter axes. The credential field absence check is the primary regression guard against leakage regressions.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-002: Trigger workspace save 100 times with 50 MB context payloads. Measure save latency. Pass criterion: 95th-percentile save latency <= 2 s.
items.rationale: The 5-desklet load is representative of the upper end of typical deployment; the measurement from OS event (not from HAUTH signal) ensures the full pipeline including OS event delivery is captured. The intent rejection check confirms the complete safe state, not just stream cessation.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-SLM-001: Issue a pairing token for device fingerprint A. Wait 61 seconds without redeeming. Attempt token redemption at T+61. Pass criterion: token rejected with TOKEN_EXPIRED. Issue fresh token; redeem within 60 s. Pass: accepted. Fail: expired token accepted or valid token rejected.
items.rationale: The 60s expiry window is the primary security control preventing token-stockpiling attacks; this test confirms the expiry is enforced at the boundary and that a fresh token still works after expiry, ruling out device-ban side effects.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RSB-128: Measure Remote SSH Boundary reconnect latency after a 5-second network interruption. Pass criterion: connection restored within 30 s with zero data loss to the command queue.
items.rationale: Testing with distinct non-allowlisted command classes confirms the rejection payload is generated from the actual gate evaluation, not from a static error template.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RSB-127: Attempt 20 SSH commands not on the allowlist. Pass criterion: all 20 rejected with COMMAND_NOT_ALLOWED; no command executed.
items.rationale: Data-minimisation properties cannot be fully tested programmatically without generating real credentials; inspection of representative records against the permitted-field list is the appropriate method.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RSB-126: Execute each of the 5 allowlisted SSH command types (status, log, restart, deploy, backup) 20 times. Measure execution latency. Pass criterion: all 100 executions complete; 95th-percentile latency <= 2 s.
items.rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.15 conformance is verified by inspection of the configured controls rather than live test, as log retention duration cannot be exercised in a test session. Derived from ARC-REQ-020 (architecture decision governing SSH Audit Logger under ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A).

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote SSH Boundary enforces a per-adapter quota of 10 commands per 60-second rolling window with 3-command burst allowance and returns a rate-limit error on quota violation.
items.rationale: Rate limiting at 10 commands per 60s rolling window with 3-command burst is testable by submitting command sequences exceeding the quota and verifying rate-limit error returns.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote SSH Boundary writes an audit record containing command text, target host, adapter identity, allowlist verdict, exit code, and duration to an append-only log for every SSH execution.
items.rationale: Audit record completeness and append-only write behaviour are testable by executing SSH commands and inspecting the audit log for required fields and write mode.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote SSH Boundary terminates SSH subprocesses after 30s of execution and enforces a maximum of 4 concurrent active SSH sessions.
items.rationale: SSH subprocess timeout at 30s and concurrent session cap at 4 are testable by running timed-out and concurrent session scenarios.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote SSH Boundary admits only allowlisted SSH commands (status, log tail, restart, deploy) and rejects all other command strings.
items.rationale: SSH command set restriction is testable by attempting non-allowlisted commands through the Remote SSH Boundary and confirming rejection.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RSB-050: Simulate SSH channel drop mid-command during 10 consecutive command sequences. Confirm automatic reconnect and command replay. Pass: all 10 sequences recover within 30 s. Fail: any sequence fails to recover or any command is lost.
items.rationale: Integration test verifying the rate-limit enforcement and quota reset cycle at the RAG-RSB interface; quota enforcement must not affect subsequent legitimate requests after the window expires.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RSB-049: Attempt 50 SSH connections from non-allowlisted hosts. Pass: all 50 rejected. Attempt 50 connections from allowlisted host. Pass: all 50 accepted. Fail: any non-allowlisted connection accepted or any allowlisted connection rejected.
items.rationale: End-to-end security test confirming allowlist enforcement at the SSH execution boundary; binary pass/fail assertable in integration test harness with SSH mock.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote Action Gateway terminates all in-progress SSH sessions within 1s of a LOCK event, rejects new requests, and returns 'LOCKED' status until unlock.
items.rationale: LOCK event handling and session termination within 1s are testable by injecting a LOCK event and measuring session teardown timing and request rejection.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote Action Result Emitter encodes each result as a JSON envelope containing status, target_host, command, stdout, stderr, exit_code, duration_ms, and adapter_id fields.
items.rationale: Result envelope JSON structure and field completeness are testable by executing commands and validating the Remote Action Result Emitter output against the schema.

items.text: Verify by Inspection that the SSH Session Manager loads SSH configuration exclusively from ~/.ssh/config, ~/.ssh/known_hosts, and host filesystem identity files with no embedded credentials in the codebase.
items.rationale: SSH configuration source path adherence (no embedded credentials) is verifiable by code inspection of the SSH Session Manager configuration loading path.

items.text: Verify by Test that the SSH Session Manager passes a ProcessResult struct to the Remote Action Result Emitter containing exit_code, stdout (max 1MiB), stderr, elapsed_ms, and truncation flag.
items.rationale: Struct field completeness and transfer integrity are verifiable by executing a command and inspecting the ProcessResult at the Remote Action Result Emitter.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote Action Gateway dispatches SSH execution commands to the Workspace Executor using the CommandEnvelope channel with adapter ID field set to 'rag'.
items.rationale: The CommandEnvelope channel reuse is verifiable by inspecting the adapter ID field set to 'rag' in dispatched commands.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Remote Action Gateway issues ActionPermitRequest to the ACG Policy Engine for every SSH command and receives a gate decision within 200ms.
items.rationale: The RAG–ACG interface reuses the ActionPermitRequest RPC, verifiable by confirming the same latency and payload constraints hold for SSH command gate calls.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RAG-041: Inject 100 malformed action payloads into the Remote Action Gateway. Pass: all 100 rejected with structured error; no payload forwarded. Fail: any payload accepted or forwarded despite malformation.
items.rationale: Tests the permit token coupling interface (IFC-RAG-001) for completeness and TOCTOU-resistance.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RAG-048 is satisfied: (1) dispatch a bounded SSH command that hangs indefinitely (tail -f /dev/null) and confirm the SSH Session Manager returns TIMEOUT result exactly 30 s ± 2 s after dispatch with exit_code = -1; (2) dispatch a long-running command (deploy category) that sleeps for 45 s and confirm the Session Manager does not terminate it at 30 s, streams stdout output via IFC-RAG-026, and returns success on completion at 45 s ± 2 s.
items.rationale: Two independent test cases are required because SUB-RAG-048 now enforces a command-class policy: a single bounded-command timeout test would not detect a regression where long-running commands are erroneously killed at 30 s, which was the defect in RT-RV-003. Both paths must be exercised to establish that the class discriminator is correctly applied at the SSH Session Manager.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-RAG-039: Submit 500 action requests to the Remote Action Gateway under sustained load. Measure request processing latency. Pass: 95th-percentile latency <= 500 ms; zero dropped requests. Fail: any latency breach or dropped request.
items.rationale: Tests the allowlist gate in both permit and deny cases, confirming the 50 ms latency and rule ID fields in the deny envelope.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Role Assignment Manager binds each paired desklet to exactly one of the six defined roles (Project, Actions, Status, Capture, Logs, AI) and rejects out-of-set binding requests.
items.rationale: Role binding to the defined six-role set and rejection of out-of-set roles are testable by attempting to bind each valid role and an invalid role to a paired desklet. The test covers all six roles mandated by SYS-REQ-007.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Device Identity Ledger persists pairing records across host restarts and rejects registrations exceeding 8 concurrent paired devices with a DEVICE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED typed error response.
items.rationale: Pairing record persistence across restarts and 8-device maximum enforcement are testable by writing 8 records, restarting, reading back, and attempting a 9th registration.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Heartbeat Monitor read query on the Device Identity Ledger returns the list of registered device identifiers and last-seen timestamps within 10ms.
items.rationale: The query latency and correctness of the Device Identity Ledger read interface are testable by querying with known registered identifiers.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Pairing–SLM Pairing Token Authority TokenValidationRequest roundtrip completes within 50ms and returns a correct permit or reject response.
items.rationale: TokenValidationRequest latency and response fields are measurable by injecting token validation calls and timing responses.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Pairing–Local Transport Server HTTP POST /pair endpoint accepts pairing requests carrying a one-time token and device fingerprint.
items.rationale: HTTP POST /pair endpoint acceptance and token embedding are testable with integration tests targeting the loopback interface.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-PAIR-032: Attempt to re-use a pairing token that has already been consumed. Pass: second use returns TOKEN_ALREADY_CONSUMED with no pairing state change. Fail: second use accepted or any state mutation occurs.
items.rationale: Tests the atomicity guarantee from IFC-PAIR-036 to confirm no partial-registration state leaks to the Heartbeat Monitor query path.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-PAIR-031: Present 100 expired pairing tokens for redemption. Pass: all 100 rejected with TOKEN_EXPIRED. Fail: any expired token accepted.
items.rationale: Directly tests the 5 s detection window from SYS-REQ-008. Testing with 3 concurrent desklets confirms that a stale path does not delay heartbeats to healthy connections, verifying the non-blocking probe requirement.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-PAIR-030: Generate a pairing token and present it from a device with a different fingerprint than the one the token was issued to. Pass: token rejected with FINGERPRINT_MISMATCH. Fail: token accepted for wrong device fingerprint.
items.rationale: Tests token uniqueness, expiry, single-use, and one-at-a-time issuance — the four security properties of SUB-PAIR-035 that prevent replay and race-condition pairing.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-KWD-125: Submit 1000 keyword detection requests with both true-positive and true-negative signal sets. Measure detection latency and accuracy. Pass criterion: 99th-percentile detection latency <= 50 ms; false-positive rate < 1%.
items.rationale: SIGKILL is the hard crash case; measuring restart-to-role-display latency and verifying no re-pair prompt appeared covers all three sub-requirements of SUB-KWD-074.

items.text: Verify by Demonstration that the Knowledge Worker Desklet Station presents an initial QR-scan pairing screen on first connection and suppresses re-pairing prompts for previously paired devices.
items.rationale: The pairing screen UX and suppression logic are best verified by operational demonstration with a real desklet device.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-KWD-054: Submit 500 context payloads to the Keyword Detector, 250 containing trigger keywords. Measure true-positive and false-positive rates. Pass: false-positive rate < 1%; all true-positive triggers detected. Fail: any false-positive rate >= 1% or any true-positive missed.
items.rationale: End-to-end test of staleness detection and reconnect behaviour using a controlled server suspension; timing assertions are derived directly from the SUB-KWD-068 thresholds.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Intent Dispatcher does not forward any intent to a downstream executor without an explicit PERMIT decision from the ACG Policy Engine under all test conditions.
items.rationale: The ACG policy-gate invariant is testable by attempting to inject intent messages that bypass the ACG check and verifying they are blocked at the Intent Dispatcher.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Intent Dispatcher–Dispatch Confirmer interface carries a dispatch-tracking record with intent request ID, target executor endpoint, and latency deadline on each dispatch.
items.rationale: Dispatch-tracking record completeness and delivery are verifiable by tracing intent execution and inspecting the record at the Dispatch Confirmer.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Conflict Serialiser releases the head-of-queue intent to the Intent Dispatcher within 1ms of the preceding intent clearing.
items.rationale: Head-of-queue release latency is testable by queuing two intents in sequence and measuring time from first-commit to second-dispatch.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Intent Dispatcher–Conflict Serialiser synchronous call returns a permit-or-queue decision within 5ms for registered intent IDs.
items.rationale: The synchronous conflict-check call latency and response structure are testable by injecting concurrent intents and measuring serialiser response time.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-INT-013: Flood the Intent Router with 10x nominal intent volume for 60 s. Measure queue depth and processing latency throughout. Pass: queue does not overflow; 95th-percentile latency stays below 200 ms throughout the flood. Fail: queue overflow or latency breach.
items.rationale: Concurrent injection against a single context object under test directly exercises the Conflict Serialiser's per-context-object queue and SUPERSEDED emission logic, confirming SYS-REQ-010 is satisfied without data races or out-of-order application.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-INT-012: Submit 1000 intents without ACG permission records. Pass: all 1000 rejected with PERMISSION_DENIED; none routed to executor. Fail: any intent without permission is routed.
items.rationale: Statistical measurement at 1000 samples provides confidence in the 95th-percentile latency bound from SYS-REQ-009; the simulated non-responding executor test specifically validates proactive TIMEOUT emission by the Dispatch Confirmer.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-INT-011: Submit 100 intents carrying valid ACG-issued permission records to the Intent Router. Pass: all 100 routed to correct Workspace Executor; zero rejected. Fail: any intent with valid permission is rejected.
items.rationale: Direct injection test at the Intent Dispatcher boundary establishes that the permission-gate check is enforced before any downstream component is invoked, satisfying the role-enforcement obligation of SYS-REQ-007.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Mode State Machine broadcasts a MODE_DEGRADED event to all registered subsystems within 500ms of SUBSYSTEM_FAILED trigger and maintains minimum performance floors during degraded mode.
items.rationale: MODE_DEGRADED event broadcast latency and minimum performance floor maintenance are testable by simulating a SUBSYSTEM_FAILED trigger and measuring broadcast timing and system capability.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HCR-017: Simulate 3 successive plugin loading failures, then restore plugin. Confirm Host Core Runtime remains in RUNNING state throughout, serving context and routing intents without interruption. Pass: zero downtime events; all 3 failures isolated. Fail: any ContextRail service interruption due to plugin failure.
items.rationale: Configuration validation is a hard boot gate for SYS-REQ-016 offline operation; negative test cases verify both the schema enforcement and the requirement that no network call is made to resolve configuration errors.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HCR-016: Crash the Host Core Runtime process. Measure time to service restoration. Pass: full service restoration within 5 s. Fail: restoration time exceeds 5 s.
items.rationale: Boot order is a safety constraint (SYS-REQ-001) verified by timing each subsystem's READY signal relative to its predecessor; the BOOT_FAILED path is a critical negative test ensuring incomplete boot does not result in a partially operational system.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HCR-015: Inject 1000 plugin API calls per second for 60 s. Measure plugin isolation breach rate and CPU overhead. Pass: zero breaches; CPU overhead < 5% above baseline. Fail: any isolation breach or overhead >= 5%.
items.rationale: The 5s detection window is directly specified in SUB-HCR-018 and is the binding constraint for SYS-REQ-008; the 100ms processing budget from IFC-HCR-014 must be verified under concurrent failure load to detect event queue saturation.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HCR-016: Inject each transition event defined in the Mode State Machine transition graph. Confirm state transitions match the graph. Pass: all transitions correct. Fail: any unexpected transition or missing transition.
items.rationale: State machine correctness can only be verified by exhaustive transition testing; illegal transition rejection must produce a log entry so that operational monitoring can detect configuration or implementation errors.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HAS-124: Measure Host Adapter Subsystem memory footprint under 100 concurrent adapter sessions. Pass criterion: RSS does not exceed 150 MB; no memory leaks after 24-hour soak.
items.rationale: Simulating HAS disconnection while monitoring operational continuity and rejected change attempts directly validates the two-property requirement: keep running + refuse changes.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Host Administration Station–ACG interface accepts structured allowlist entry objects and rejects malformed entries with typed error codes identifying the specific validation failure.
items.rationale: Allowlist entry submission and rejection of malformed entries are testable via Host Administration Station integration tests.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-HAS-053: Execute 200 adapter invocations across 5 adapter types. For each, verify: invocation completes within the adapter timeout; result returned to Intent Router; adapter sandbox cannot access host filesystem outside designated working dir. Pass: all 200 pass all three checks. Fail: any sandbox escape or timeout breach.
items.rationale: Verifies mode-gated access control for allowlist modifications; both paths (rejected and accepted) confirm the gate is active and mode-sensitive.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Command Dispatcher routes CommandEnvelopes with empty or 'local' adapter ID to Process Supervisor and 'rag' adapter ID to Remote Action Gateway.
items.rationale: Command routing via adapter ID field ('local', 'rag') is testable by dispatching CommandEnvelopes with each adapter ID value and confirming the correct execution path.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Process Supervisor captures the first 4KB of combined stdout/stderr, computes a SHA-256 digest of the captured output, and passes the digest and truncation flag to the Outcome Reporter.
items.rationale: Stdout/stderr capture limit and SHA-256 digest generation are testable by executing a process with known output and inspecting the captured digest and truncation flag.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Process Supervisor passes a ProcessResult struct to the Outcome Reporter containing exit code, status, stdout digest, truncation flag, and elapsed time.
items.rationale: The ProcessResult struct fields and pass-through integrity are verifiable by tracing execution through the Process Supervisor and validating the Outcome Reporter output.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Command Dispatcher passes a fully-populated execution request struct to the Process Supervisor with required path, argument vector, environment, and timeout fields present.
items.rationale: Struct field completeness and execution routing are verifiable by injecting CommandDispatcher requests and validating the struct received at Process Supervisor.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EXE-022: Queue 100 intents. Crash the Workspace Executor mid-queue. Restart the executor. Confirm queue is fully replayed. Pass: all 100 intents re-processed after restart. Fail: any intent lost or duplicated.
items.rationale: IFC-EXE-017 mandates the Executor returns CommandResult within 200 ms of process exit; this end-to-end measurement verifies the full Dispatcher-Supervisor-Reporter chain latency including event bus scheduling overhead.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EXE-021: Submit 50 intent batches each containing 10 parallel actions. Measure time from batch submission to all actions complete. Pass: 95th-percentile batch completion time <= 500 ms. Fail: any batch exceeds 500 ms.
items.rationale: Sequential execution is a correctness guarantee for context model consistency; this test must catch any async or thread-pool path that could produce concurrent subprocess invocations not visible to a single-invocation test.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EXE-020: Inject 10 concurrent intent execution attempts for the same exclusive resource. Pass: exactly one succeeds; all others receive RESOURCE_CONFLICT within 100 ms. Fail: more than one succeeds or any other receives a non-RESOURCE_CONFLICT error.
items.rationale: The 50 ms SLA in IFC-EXE-018 is measurable end-to-end; 100 iterations give a meaningful p95 estimate. This test exercises both the Outcome Reporter publication timing and the event bus subscriber delivery pipeline.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EXE-022 is satisfied: inject a CommandEnvelope targeting a sleep-forever subprocess; confirm the Process Supervisor returns a 5-second execution-started acknowledgement within 200 ms of receipt, delivers SIGKILL to the process group between 5000 ms and 5100 ms after subprocess launch, leaves no surviving child processes, and returns CommandResult with status TIMEOUT and elapsed time greater than or equal to 5000 ms.
items.rationale: SUB-EXE-022 separates the 200 ms acknowledgement window (UI responsiveness, SYS-REQ-009) from the 5-second wall-clock execution timeout; the test must verify both independent constraints. Process-group targeting is verified explicitly because an implementation that kills only the direct child but not grandchildren would pass a naive timeout check while still accumulating orphaned subprocesses.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EXE-018: Execute 1000 intents across all 5 intent types under nominal load. Measure execution latency. Pass: 95th-percentile latency <= 500 ms. Fail: 95th-percentile latency exceeds 500 ms.
items.rationale: Permit-gate bypass is a security regression; this test must run at every build. The two-path structure confirms both the deny and allow branches of the validation guard.

items.text: Verify by Test that External Application Boundary BASIC adapter actions pass through ACG gate evaluation before execution and that the action set is confined to launch, URL open, local script, and clipboard.
items.rationale: BASIC adapter mediation for application launch and URL open are testable by executing known actions and confirming ACG gate evaluation occurs.

items.text: Verify by Test that the External Application Boundary–Adapter Framework interface returns a normalised action result within 6s including a 5s callback timeout plus 1s processing margin.
items.rationale: The 6s total roundtrip with 5s callback timeout is measurable in integration tests by dispatching action requests and instrumenting end-to-end latency.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-EAB-052: Execute 100 BASIC adapter invocations (launch, URL-open, script, keyboard, restore) across all 5 types. Pass: all succeed and their effects are observable in the target application; none bypass the allowlist gate. Fail: any invocation fails or any bypasses allowlist gate.
items.rationale: Security test confirming the RFC 1918 restriction blocks non-local callbacks; observable via network capture with no egress traffic during the test.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Web Boundary relays role-scoped context updates to connected desklets within 50ms of publication and that no out-of-scope context objects are transmitted.
items.rationale: Context update relay latency and role-scope enforcement are testable by instrumenting Desklet Web Boundary output and verifying scope filtering under load.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Web Boundary WebSocket interface carries only role-scoped context and high-level intent frames and rejects connections from unpaired devices.
items.rationale: WebSocket transport and role-scope filtering are end-to-end testable with a spare device connecting as a desklet and receiving context payloads inspected for scope compliance.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Web Boundary–Intent Router interface accepts valid intent JSON frames and returns a dispatch confirmation within 500ms with required correlation fields.
items.rationale: Intent frame acceptance, validation, and dispatch confirmation are verifiable by submitting valid and invalid intent frames and checking response structure and timing.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DWB-051: Establish 20 WSS connections from desklets to the Desklet Web Boundary. Send 1000 context update events across the pool. Pass: all 1000 events delivered; TLS 1.3 confirmed by protocol inspector; zero credential fields in any message. Fail: any event lost or any non-WSS/non-TLS-1.3 connection accepted.
items.rationale: Verifies the WebSocket authentication gate; both positive and negative paths are required to confirm the security boundary is enforced.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Deep Integration Boundary terminates a DEEP adapter session within 10s of last heartbeat, releases subscriptions, and notifies the Adapter Framework.
items.rationale: Session termination after heartbeat timeout is testable by suppressing heartbeat messages and measuring the time to session teardown and Adapter Framework notification.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Deep Integration Boundary routes workspace context events to subscribed DEEP adapters within 50ms of event publication and enforces per-adapter rate limiting.
items.rationale: Context event delivery latency and per-adapter rate-limiting are testable by subscribing an instrumented DEEP adapter and measuring event delivery timing under load.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Deep Integration Boundary accepts DEEP adapter connections over a Unix domain socket and completes the capability-grant handshake within 500ms of connection.
items.rationale: Unix domain socket acceptance and capability-grant handshake timing are testable by connecting a simulated DEEP adapter and measuring handshake completion.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Deep Integration Boundary confines each DEEP adapter to its declared capability scope and rejects out-of-scope context access requests.
items.rationale: DEEP integration confinement to per-adapter scope is testable by attempting out-of-scope context access from an instrumented DEEP adapter.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Deep Integration Boundary receives updated capability scope policies from ACG within 2s of an allowlist change event.
items.rationale: Capability scope policy propagation latency is testable by modifying an allowlist entry and measuring delivery time at the Deep Integration Boundary.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DIB-048: Attempt 50 Deep Integration Boundary data exchanges outside the adapter's registered capability set. Pass: all 50 rejected with CAPABILITY_EXCEEDED. Fail: any exchange outside capability set is accepted.
items.rationale: Security test verifying capability scope enforcement; pass/fail is binary and automatically assertable in integration test harness.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DIB-047: Exchange 500 structured workspace context payloads across 3 deep integration types (MCP, browser extension, IDE extension). Pass: all 500 delivered intact with zero payload corruption. Fail: any payload corrupted or not delivered.
items.rationale: Verifies event routing latency and rate-limiting at the DIB-Workspace Context Store interface; both properties are testable in a controlled integration harness.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DIB-046: Establish MCP server, browser extension, and IDE extension deep integrations. Verify each uses its registered API channel only. Pass: all three integrations functional; none can access another adapter's channel. Fail: any cross-adapter channel access.
items.rationale: Integration test at the DIB-Adapter Framework boundary; verifies the DEEP adapter protocol response latency meets the action-roundtrip budget.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Context Feed Client processes role-scoped context updates at up to 10Hz and delivers each update to the Desklet Shell Renderer within 50ms of receipt.
items.rationale: Context update processing rate and delivery latency are measurable by sending context updates at 10Hz and instrumenting delivery timing to the Desklet Shell Renderer.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Desklet Client Framework resumes normal rendering within 3s of receiving a host resume command, with context feed re-subscription confirmed.
items.rationale: Resume latency from host resume command to normal rendering is measurable by timing the resume sequence with context re-subscription verification.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Connection Health Monitor delivers disconnection and reconnection events to the Lifecycle State Machine within 200ms of detection with required payload fields.
items.rationale: Disconnection detection and event delivery latency are measurable by simulating WebSocket disconnects and measuring delivery to the Lifecycle State Machine.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Lifecycle State Machine delivers active/quiesced/resuming state transition commands to the Desklet Shell Renderer via the event bus within 100ms.
items.rationale: State transition command latency and field completeness are testable by triggering lifecycle transitions and instrumenting the event bus receiver.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Context Feed Client delivers context update events to the Desklet Shell Renderer via the in-process event bus within 50ms of WebSocket receipt.
items.rationale: In-process event delivery latency is measurable by timestamping at WebSocket receipt and at Desklet Shell Renderer receive.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DCF-045: Verify all 3 configuration paths (local-only, encrypted-remote, hybrid) persist and restore their respective secrets/preferences without cross-contamination. Pass: each path restores only its own data. Fail: any cross-contamination or data loss.
items.rationale: Validates the end-to-end detection and notification chain for the most critical failure mode (loss of host connectivity), confirming both the 10s detection ceiling and the 200ms IFC delivery budget.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DCF-044: Introduce a corrupted config block. Confirm detection and automatic rollback within 1 s. Pass: rollback completes within 1 s; service remains available. Fail: rollback takes > 1 s or service becomes unavailable.
items.rationale: Sustained throughput test at rated frequency validates that neither the Local Transport Server nor the WebSocket connection drops messages under load. 60s duration catches any periodic loss pattern (e.g., GC pauses).

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DCF-043: Read and write config 1000 times concurrently (500 reads, 500 writes). Pass: zero torn reads; all writes reflected in subsequent reads. Fail: any torn read or lost write.
items.rationale: End-to-end quiesce test from host command to display blank validates the full latency path through the Lifecycle State Machine and Desklet Shell Renderer.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-DCF-042: Read config 1000 times under nominal load. Measure read latency. Pass: 99th-percentile latency <= 10 ms. Fail: any read exceeds 10 ms at 99th percentile.
items.rationale: Direct latency measurement from command injection to visible render covers the full rendering path and validates the SUB-DCF-051 acceptance criterion.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Workspace Context Store rebuilds the authoritative context after a host process restart: establish a full workspace context, terminate the host process, restart it, and verify that (a) all pairing records are restored from the Device Identity Ledger within 2 s, (b) all context attributes are marked stale immediately after restart, and (c) stale markers clear for each integration within 30 ms of the first successful event from that integration.
items.rationale: SUB-CTX-080 specifies a 2-second rebuild window and stale marking on restart; the test validates the rebuild timeline from a real crash scenario using Device Identity Ledger (SUB-CTX-036) and confirms the stale-clearing mechanism works per integration, preventing desklets from acting on outdated pre-crash context.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Event Bus Adapter enforces a 200-event ingestion queue bound: inject 250 rapid workspace events faster than the registry write rate, confirm that (a) no more than 200 are queued, (b) a ContextOverflow notification is emitted within 10 ms of the 201st event, and (c) the registry state reflects the 50 most recent events rather than the 50 oldest.
items.rationale: SUB-CTX-079 specifies a drop-oldest eviction policy with a ContextOverflow notification; the test validates the specific drop count (200 cap), the notification timing (10 ms), and the eviction direction (oldest dropped, newest retained), confirming backpressure is effective and observable.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Workspace Context Store continues serving the last successfully applied context snapshot to all paired desklets at >= 10 Hz during Degraded mode, with stale-attribute marking visible in projected payloads.
items.rationale: Degraded mode context serving and update delivery at full rate are testable by simulating host integration failure and measuring context delivery continuity.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Event Bus Adapter subscribes to host workspace events (tool open/close, window focus, project switch, command outcome) and translates each into a Context Object Record within 200ms.
items.rationale: Event subscription and context-object record update latency are testable by injecting workspace events and measuring the time to context record update.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Context Object Registry exposes a read API returning the full workspace snapshot to the Role Scope Filter within 50ms with consistent per-read timestamps.
items.rationale: The read API latency and snapshot consistency guarantees are testable under concurrent load by measuring response time and comparing snapshot timestamps.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Workspace Context Store delivers SSE context updates to the Desklet Client Framework within 200ms of workspace state change and that no out-of-scope context is included.
items.rationale: SSE delivery latency and role-scope filtering are verifiable by establishing a desklet client connection and measuring event delivery timing with scope assertions.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Workspace Context Store publishes a ContextUpdated event to the Intent Router in-process bus within 100ms of a workspace event with required fields present.
items.rationale: The in-process publish-subscribe event latency is measurable by instrumenting the event bus with timestamps at publish and at Intent Router receive.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-CTX-029: Inject 100 context write attempts from processes not registered as context providers. Pass: all 100 rejected with UNAUTHORIZED_WRITER. Fail: any unauthorized write accepted.
items.rationale: Non-blocking delivery per IFC-CTX-035 is the primary isolation guarantee for desklet-fabric resilience. Testing at the 8-desklet ceiling with a simulated stall directly exercises the per-connection isolation path and validates that the worst-case single-connection failure does not degrade the streaming path for other desklets.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-CTX-028: Disconnect the Context Object Store from its persistence layer mid-operation. Measure time to re-establish consistency. Pass: consistency restored within 5 s with no data loss. Fail: data loss or consistency not restored within 5 s.
items.rationale: Boundary testing of the write API validates both the latency contract and the rejection semantics for malformed inputs. The absence of partial writes under rejection confirms the atomic write guarantee that prevents inconsistent registry state during ingestion failures.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-CTX-027: Inject 1000 context updates concurrently. Measure conflict resolution time and read-your-write latency. Pass: conflict resolution < 10 ms; read-your-write satisfied within 50 ms. Fail: any conflict unresolved or read-your-write latency > 50 ms.
items.rationale: The timing assertions directly exercise the safety requirement in SYS-REQ-003. Testing with 3 concurrent desklets confirms the gate operates per-subsystem rather than per-connection, and the zero-delivery-in-locked condition confirms no partial or buffered context leaks during the locked state.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-CTX-026: Measure context read and write latency under nominal load with 10 concurrent context consumers. Pass: read latency <= 50 ms at 99th percentile; write latency <= 100 ms at 99th percentile. Fail: either latency threshold exceeded.
items.rationale: Testing each of the six canonical roles with a real paired desklet verifies both filter correctness and delivery latency under actual push-path conditions, covering the information-boundary enforcement that SYS-REQ-011 requires. The role set {Project, Actions, Status, Capture, Logs, AI} is defined by SYS-REQ-007 and STK-REQ-003.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-CTX-030: Inject 100 sequential write operations into the Context Object Store. After each write, read the value back. Pass: every read returns the most recently written value; zero stale reads. Fail: any stale read detected.
items.rationale: Direct latency measurement of the write path under realistic load validates the 50 ms budget without relying on synthetic microbenchmarks. 100 writes covers the history depth limit to verify graceful eviction.

items.text: Verify by Test that the External Application Boundary enforces BASIC adapter execution sandboxing: (a) attempt a local script execution targeting a path outside the adapter's declared scope — verify failure with a typed error and no file access; (b) attempt keyboard simulation while a privileged system dialog is in the foreground — verify the keystroke is not delivered to the dialog. Pass criterion: both containment checks satisfied.
items.rationale: SUB-ADP-082 specifies path-scoped script execution and foreground-window keyboard containment; the test validates both constraints under adversarial invocations to confirm the sandbox cannot be breached by a misconfigured or malicious adapter at runtime.

items.text: Verify by Test that the External Application Boundary enforces BASIC adapter manifest signature verification at registration: (a) register an adapter with a valid manifest signed by a trusted key — verify acceptance; (b) register an adapter with no manifest signature — verify rejection; (c) register an adapter with a signature from an untrusted key — verify rejection with a typed error. Pass criterion: all three outcomes observed.
items.rationale: SUB-ADP-081 requires cryptographic manifest signature verification at registration; the three-case test covers the positive path and both rejection paths, confirming the trust gate is not bypassable by an unsigned or wrongly-signed adapter.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ADP-123: Deploy 5 adapter types simultaneously. Measure memory footprint per adapter under 20 concurrent invocations each. Pass criterion: per-adapter memory footprint <= 50 MB; no cross-adapter memory leakage.
items.rationale: Process restart with immediate lookup covers the persistence and recovery path; adapter count equality before and after restart is the objective measure of durable state.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ADP-122: Invoke 100 adapter rollbacks. Measure rollback completion time. Pass criterion: 95th-percentile rollback latency <= 500 ms; zero partial-state residues.
items.rationale: SIGKILL is the hardest failure mode (no cleanup); timing from kill to broker-ready readiness and intent completion validates both the supervisor restart budget and the queue-then-resubmit path.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ADP-121: Deploy 20 adapters to the Adapter Framework using the standard registration API. Invoke each 50 times. Pass criterion: all 20 adapters deployed successfully; all 1000 invocations complete without cross-adapter interference.
items.rationale: Direct fault injection against the manifest file is the only reliable method to exercise the last-good-cache fallback path without restarting the host process.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Adapter Manifest Loader rejects manifests failing schema validation, referencing non-existent executables, or exceeding permission scope, with structured error responses identifying the specific rejection reason.
items.rationale: Manifest schema validation and rejection of invalid or referencing non-existent executables is testable by supplying malformed manifests and confirming rejection with error codes.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Adapter Manifest Loader atomically replaces Adapter Registry contents on hot-reload with no intermediate state visible to concurrent Action Broker queries.
items.rationale: Atomic bulk-write behaviour under hot-reload is testable by triggering a reload while a concurrent lookup is in progress and verifying no partial state is exposed.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Action Broker–Adapter Registry synchronous lookup returns the resolved executable path and permission scope within 5ms for registered adapters.
items.rationale: Lookup latency and return structure are directly testable by calling the registry with a known adapter ID and timing the response.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Adapter Framework dispatches a fully-populated AdaptedCommand to the Workspace Executor with all required fields present and that the executor accepts it without schema rejection.
items.rationale: The typed AdaptedCommand message can be validated structurally by inspection and behaviourally by end-to-end dispatch tests checking field completeness and routing.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Adapter Framework–ACG Policy Engine synchronous RPC roundtrip completes within 50ms and returns a correctly structured ActionPermitResponse.
items.rationale: The synchronous RPC call is exercised in integration tests by dispatching an ActionPermitRequest and measuring roundtrip latency with the permit/deny field validated.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ADP-024: Register 5 adapters with conflicting capability claims. Pass: registration system rejects conflicts with CAPABILITY_CONFLICT. Fail: any conflicting adapter pair registered without error.
items.rationale: Directory-tree path enforcement is a security boundary; both the registration rejection and the downstream lookup failure must be verified to confirm the defence-in-depth chain.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ADP-023: Invoke 100 adapter executions simultaneously. Measure total execution time versus sequential baseline. Pass: parallel execution is at least 3x faster than sequential baseline. Fail: speedup ratio < 3x.
items.rationale: The permit gate is a security control; both the deny and allow paths must be tested, and the absence of execution on DENY must be explicitly verified rather than inferred from the response status.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ACG-129: Perform 24-hour soak test of the Allowlist and Configuration Governance subsystem under nominal load. Pass criterion: zero memory growth > 10 MB; zero CPU drift > 5%; all policy decisions correct throughout.
items.rationale: IEC 62443-3-3 SR 2.1 conformance is assessed by inspecting the policy documentation and change attribution records; direct test of the standard's detailed control requirements is impractical in a bounded test session.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Allowlist Store persists allowlist entries atomically such that process restart or power cycle produces no partial writes and all entries are identically recoverable.
items.rationale: Atomic persistence is testable by writing allowlist entries, simulating a process kill, restarting, and verifying entry integrity and completeness.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Maintenance Configuration Interface delivers a mode-change event to Host Core Runtime within 500ms of trigger, carrying the correct new mode value.
items.rationale: Mode-change event propagation latency and payload fidelity are verifiable by injecting a mode-change trigger and instrumenting the receiving subsystem.

items.text: Verify by Test that the Policy Engine–Remote Action Gateway gate evaluation interface delivers a synchronous response containing permit/deny and audit metadata within 200ms under nominal load.
items.rationale: The interface latency and payload structure are directly measurable in integration tests by injecting gate evaluation requests from RAG and recording response times and field presence.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ACG-010: With ContextRail in RUNNING mode, invoke the allowlist reload API 20 times in rapid succession. Pass: all 20 reloads complete without service interruption; final allowlist state correct. Fail: any service interruption during reload.
items.rationale: End-to-end test of the Adapter Framework gate API verifies the interface contract specified in IFC-ACG-008, exercising both allow and deny paths and confirming latency compliance at the integration boundary.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ACG-009: With ContextRail in non-Maintenance mode, attempt to add, remove, and modify 3 allowlist entries each. Pass: all 9 operations rejected with MODE_RESTRICTION. Fail: any modification succeeds outside Maintenance mode.
items.rationale: Mode-gating verification exercises both reject and accept paths of the mode guard, confirming SYS-REQ-005 compliance. State-change inspection of the Allowlist Store confirms that mode-rejection does not result in partial mutations.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ACG-008: Submit 100 action requests not present in the Allowlist Store. After each rejection, inspect the Allowlist Audit Logger. Pass criterion: for each rejected request, a corresponding log entry exists with matching action identifier, correct principal identity, UTC timestamp within 10 ms of the gate decision, and reason code DENY_NOT_LISTED; no log entry is missing.
items.rationale: Verifies that the audit trail is complete and accurate for every denial — the primary evidence requirement from SYS-REQ-004. Timestamp within 10 ms bounds confirms the log is written atomically with the gate decision rather than asynchronously.

items.text: Verify by Test that SUB-ACG-007: Submit 1000 action evaluation requests to the Policy Engine under nominal CPU load with the Allowlist Store populated to 500 entries. Measure gate decision latency from request submission to response receipt. Pass criterion: 99th-percentile latency <= 5 ms; zero requests return a result other than ALLOW or DENY.
items.rationale: Latency measurement under 500-entry load exercises the worst-case linear scan of the allowlist; 1000-sample population gives statistically meaningful 99th-percentile. Both ALLOW and DENY responses must be exercised to confirm completeness of the gate path.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL provide all core functions — pairing, role rendering, context streaming, intent execution, and allowlisted actions — without any outbound internet connectivity.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-011/STK-REQ-012 (INV-01); FBS function 'manage host and mode lifecycle'. Local-first operation is the property that keeps execution off any third-party cloud.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL execute remote SSH actions using the host operator's existing SSH configuration and SHALL pass each remote command through the allowlist gate before dispatch.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-009/STK-REQ-010; FBS function 'broker remote SSH action'. Reusing existing SSH config creates no new standing credential, and routing through the gate enforces the allowlisted command set.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL broker external application actions only through registered BASIC or DEEP adapters and SHALL confine each adapter to its registered capability set.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-007/STK-REQ-008 (INV-04); FBS functions 'broker external application action' and 'register integration adapter'. Capability-bounded adapters are how API-optional integration stays inside the execution boundary.

items.text: While in Degraded mode, the ContextRail SHALL continue rendering the last-known role context on each surviving desklet with a staleness of no more than 30 s, SHALL annotate that context as stale, and SHALL reject intents on any failed path with an explicit status rather than dropping them silently.
items.rationale: Resolves OQ-03 (Degraded-mode performance floor) and realises STK-REQ-002 across the Degraded mode; FBS functions 'isolate failed desklet path' and 'quiesce and resume desklets'. The 30 s staleness bound and stale-annotation prevent a surviving desklet from presenting confidently-wrong state during partial failure.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL maintain a single authoritative workspace context model on the host and SHALL propagate a context change to all subscribed desklets within 200 ms under nominal load.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-002/STK-REQ-003; FBS function 'maintain workspace context model'. A single host-authoritative model avoids cross-desklet divergence; 200 ms keeps all surfaces coherent within one interaction beat.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL stream to each desklet only the subset of workspace context scoped to that desklet's assigned role.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-003/STK-REQ-004; FBS function 'stream role-scoped context to desklets'. Role-scoping limits the confidentiality exposure of any single device to only what its role needs.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL serialise conflicting intents that target the same workspace context object, apply them in receipt order, and report rejection with status to any desklet whose intent is superseded.
items.rationale: Resolves OQ-02 (concurrency) and realises STK-REQ-002; FBS functions 'execute workspace command' and 'report action outcome'. Receipt-order serialisation gives deterministic context consistency when multiple desklets act at once.

items.text: When a Knowledge Worker dispatches an intent from a desklet over the local network, the ContextRail SHALL reflect execution status on the originating desklet within 200 ms at the 95th percentile under nominal load.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-002 and the interactivity constraint; FBS functions 'receive and route desklet intent', 'confirm intent dispatch', 'report action outcome'. 200 ms p95 is the documented provisional interactivity floor for the round-trip on a LAN; beyond it the surface stops feeling like a control.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL detect loss of a desklet link within 5 s via heartbeat and SHALL restore the desklet's prior role binding on reconnect within the same session without requiring re-pairing.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-001; FBS functions 'monitor desklet liveness' and 're-bind desklet role on reconnect'. 5 s detection bounds how long a dead surface shows stale context before Degraded handling engages.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL bind each desklet to exactly one role from {Project, Actions, Status, Capture, Logs, AI} and SHALL reject any intent that falls outside that role's capability set.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-003; FBS functions 'render assigned desklet role' and 'receive and route desklet intent'. One-role-per-desklet is both the UX model and a least-privilege control on what each surface can request.

items.text: The ContextRail host SHALL serve the desklet web surface over a local WebSocket transport and SHALL admit a successfully paired desklet to render its assigned role within 3 s of join completion.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-001/STK-REQ-003; FBS function 'serve desklet web surface'. 3 s is the join-to-usable budget that keeps repurposing a spare device feeling immediate rather than like a setup chore.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL allow the Workstation Owner to add, remove, and review allowlist entries only via the host while in Maintenance mode, and such changes SHALL take effect without exposing the allowlist surface to any desklet.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-005 and resolves OQ-04 (allowlist governance); FBS function 'govern allowlist and adapter configuration'. Confining edits to host Maintenance keeps the governance surface off the untrusted desklet tier.

items.text: The ContextRail SHALL evaluate every host-mediated external and remote action against the active allowlist and SHALL deny and log any action not explicitly present in the allowlist.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-005/STK-REQ-009; FBS function 'enforce action allowlist'. Default-deny with audit is the only enforceable form of the execution boundary across BASIC, DEEP, and SSH action paths.

items.text: When the operator locks the host, the ContextRail SHALL within 1 s cease streaming context to all desklets and reject all subsequent intents, while keeping the action allowlist enforced, until the operator re-authenticates at the host.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-006 and the Locked safe-state mode; FBS function 'enforce lock safe-state'. 1 s bounds the window in which context remains visible on an unattended desklet after the lock command.

items.text: When a desklet joins, the ContextRail host SHALL authenticate the desklet with a single-use pairing token bound to that device for the session, SHALL reject any message bearing an unrecognised or expired token, and SHALL expire an unredeemed pairing token within 60 s of issue.
items.rationale: Resolves OQ-01 (pairing trust model) and realises STK-REQ-001/STK-REQ-004; FBS functions 'pair desklet via QR code' and 'authenticate host operator'. A bounded-lifetime single-use token is the minimum to stop a captured QR/URL from admitting an attacker device; 60 s balances usability against replay exposure on a LAN.

items.text: The ContextRail host SHALL store all credentials and SHALL execute all sensitive workspace and remote actions exclusively within the host process; no credential or executable capability SHALL be transmitted to or persisted on any desklet.
items.rationale: Realises STK-REQ-004 (INV-02/INV-03) and FBS function 'protect streamed context confidentiality'. The single-point-of-execution invariant is the core confidentiality property; any leak of a credential or capability onto a user-owned device collapses the entire boundary.

items.text: The Local Transport Server SHALL operate within a steady-state host resource budget of at most 150 MB resident memory and at most 5% average CPU utilisation on a single host CPU core when serving up to 6 concurrent desklet connections at 10 Hz context update rate.
items.rationale: The Local Transport Server is a Node.js process running on the knowledge-worker's workstation; its resource envelope must not degrade the user's primary work applications. The 150 MB RSS limit is derived from typical Node.js WebSocket server baselines; the 5% CPU bound reflects the mostly-idle event-loop character of a WebSocket relay. These bounds govern the host's power budget allocated to ContextRail and close the Powered-trait completeness gap (RT-GAP-001) flagged against UHT hex {{hex:50E57108}}.

items.text: While in Degraded mode (one or more context update failures), the Local Transport Server SHALL continue accepting connections, routing heartbeat pings at no less than 1 Hz per client, and delivering the last successfully received context frame to each connected desklet within 200 ms of a new connection, maintaining all active WebSocket connections for at least 60 seconds after the last successful upstream context update.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-013 (Degraded mode: last-known context). The 1 Hz heartbeat floor prevents desklet timeout disconnection; the 60 s window matches the upstream reconnect retry budget; the 200 ms delivery bound keeps the desklet UI coherent on join. The transport layer must remain operational during degraded context operation so desklets stay visually coherent and heartbeat continuity prevents unnecessary role-restoration churn during transient upstream failures.

items.text: When the Local Transport Server receives a LOCK event from the Security and Lock Manager, it SHALL close all active WebSocket connections within 1 s and SHALL reject all subsequent WebSocket upgrade requests until an UNLOCK event is received.
items.rationale: Directly derived from SYS-REQ-003 (cease streaming on lock within 1 s). The transport layer is the enforcement point for the lock safe-state — closing sockets here guarantees no context frames can reach desklets regardless of the state of upstream components.

items.text: The Local Transport Server's HTTP Static Asset Server SHALL serve the desklet web surface bundle (HTML, bundled JS, CSS) over HTTPS on the same port as the WebSocket Gateway, using content-hash cache headers, and SHALL complete the initial HTTP response within 300 ms on the local network.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-006 (serve desklet web surface). Content-hash caching eliminates redundant transfers after the first load. 300 ms budget is set by human perception thresholds for initial page load on a local LAN; exceeding this makes the pairing flow feel unresponsive.

items.text: The Local Transport Server's Channel Multiplexer SHALL deliver each context delta frame issued by the Workspace Context Store to all registered desklet connections whose assigned role matches the frame's role scope, with end-to-end dispatch latency ≤ 5 ms for up to 16 simultaneous connections under peak update rate.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-011 (role-scoped context streaming) and SYS-REQ-016 (all core functions on local network). 5 ms dispatch latency at 16 connections preserves the interactive responsiveness of the desklet UI; exceeding this budget introduces perceptible lag in context updates.

items.text: The Local Transport Server's Heartbeat Monitor SHALL send a WebSocket ping frame to each active desklet connection every 2 s and SHALL fire a DISCONNECT event to the WebSocket Gateway if no pong response is received within 5 s of the most recent ping, removing the connection from the Connection Registry within 100 ms of the event.
items.rationale: Directly derives from SYS-REQ-008 (5 s detection window). The 2 s ping interval provides two missed-pong opportunities within the 5 s budget, reducing false disconnects due to transient jitter. 100 ms registry removal prevents stale entries from routing further context frames to a dead socket.

items.text: The Local Transport Server SHALL admit a connecting desklet to the WebSocket Gateway only after verifying the single-use pairing token presented at the initial HTTP upgrade request, and SHALL close the socket with a 401 status within 200 ms if the token is absent or invalid.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-002 (authentication) and SYS-REQ-006 (WebSocket admission). The 200 ms limit prevents connection-slot exhaustion from unauthenticated probes; single-use token enforcement prevents replay attacks on a shared LAN.

items.text: The Local Transport Server SHALL bind the WebSocket Gateway to a configurable host and port and SHALL serve all active desklet connections over TLS using a host-generated self-signed certificate rotated on each host process restart.
items.rationale: SYS-REQ-006 mandates a local WebSocket transport for the desklet web surface. TLS is required to prevent credential interception on shared LAN segments; self-signed with per-restart rotation eliminates certificate distribution overhead while bounding the validity window of any compromised certificate.

items.text: The Security and Lock Manager SHALL ensure that no credential, authentication token, or executable capability is included in any outbound context payload or pairing response transmitted to a desklet.
items.rationale: Directly implements SYS-REQ-001. Credentials transmitted to a desklet would be stored in the browser/PWA runtime memory, outside the host trust boundary. Inspection of the outbound payload schema plus integration test with a credential-injected context object provides dual assurance.

items.text: The Context Access Guard SHALL filter every outbound context snapshot to include only attributes tagged with the requesting desklet's assigned role, and SHALL block the entire snapshot if any attribute cannot be role-classified, before the payload is passed to the transport layer.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-011. The fail-closed behaviour (block on unclassified attribute) is required because an unclassified attribute may contain credentials or cross-role information; permitting delivery would violate the confidentiality model. Role classification is static at role-bind time so unclassified attributes indicate a model inconsistency.

items.text: The Lock State Controller SHALL, upon receiving a lock event, cease streaming context to all active desklet sessions and begin rejecting all inbound intents within 1000 ms of the event timestamp, regardless of the number of active desklet sessions.
items.rationale: Directly implements SYS-REQ-003. The 1s wall-clock budget is a safety-of-information constraint: context streamed after a workstation is locked is visible to anyone at the desklet screen. No relaxation is permitted for session count — the broadcast quiesce must be parallel, not sequential.

items.text: The Host Authenticator SHALL detect an OS session-lock event and signal the Lock State Controller within 200 ms of the OS event timestamp.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-003 which requires the full lock safe-state (cessation of streaming) within 1s. The 200ms budget for event detection leaves 800ms for the LSC to quiesce all active desklet streams, accommodating up to 8 concurrent sessions at 100ms each.

items.text: The Pairing Token Authority SHALL reject, with no retry permitted, any join message bearing an unrecognised, already-redeemed, or expired token, and SHALL log the rejection with the desklet fingerprint and timestamp.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-002. A used or expired token is evidence of replay or race-condition attack; logging the fingerprint and timestamp supports forensic review of pairing anomalies.

items.text: The Pairing Token Authority SHALL issue a cryptographically unique single-use pairing token bound to the requesting desklet device fingerprint, and SHALL expire any unredeemed token within 60 seconds of issue.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-002. The 60s window matches the SYS requirement floor; shorter windows risk failure on slow-join devices; longer windows expose the host to token-replay attacks. The device-fingerprint binding prevents token reuse from a different desklet.

items.text: When the SSH Command Gate rejects an SSH action because the command is not on the allowlist, the Remote SSH Boundary SHALL return a rejection response that identifies the denied command class and the allowlist reference that blocked it, and SHALL provide the adapter with a machine-readable rejection reason code, so that the adapter developer can distinguish an allowlist gap from a permission error.
items.rationale: The SSH Command Gate (UHT: Ethically Significant, hex 41E53909) controls which SSH commands may be executed via ContextRail; opaque rejections ('access denied') without cause identification create a disproportionate power asymmetry — the adapter developer cannot determine whether the gate is misconfigured or the command is genuinely prohibited. Structured rejection responses support operator transparency and allow proportionate governance of the allowlist without requiring privileged access to the host configuration.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary SHALL log only the command text, target host, adapter identity, allowlist verdict, execution duration, and result code for each SSH action; it SHALL NOT log SSH credentials, private key material, or the content of any files retrieved via SSH, and the audit log SHALL be accessible only to the host operator role.
items.rationale: The SSH Audit Logger (UHT: Ethically Significant, hex 40853349) records sensitive operator actions; collecting more data than is needed for security accountability — such as file contents or credentials — would constitute disproportionate surveillance contrary to the data-minimisation principle (GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) and equivalent frameworks). Restricting access to the host operator role prevents audit data from being used for secondary purposes without appropriate authorisation.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary audit log SHALL conform to the audit record requirements of ISO/IEC 27001 (Information security management systems — Requirements) Annex A control 8.15 (Logging), retaining each SSH command audit record for a minimum of 90 days and protecting log integrity against modification or deletion.
items.rationale: The SSH Audit Logger (UHT: Institutionally Defined, hex 40853349) captures operator actions over an authenticated SSH channel; ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.15 mandates that audit logs are protected from tampering and retained for a period sufficient to support incident investigation. 90-day retention aligns with typical security-incident investigation timescales for an enterprise knowledge-work platform.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary SHALL enforce a per-adapter SSH command quota of 10 commands per 60-second rolling window, with a burst allowance of 3 commands, and SHALL return a rate-limit error on quota violation without executing the command.
items.rationale: Rate limiting prevents a compromised or runaway DEEP adapter from using ContextRail as a force-multiplier for SSH command flooding on remote hosts; the quota values are conservative defaults that cover legitimate automation while bounding worst-case abuse.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary SHALL write an audit record containing command text, target host, adapter identity, allowlist verdict, exit code, and execution duration to an append-only log for every SSH execution attempt, including denied attempts.
items.rationale: Audit completeness is required by SYS-REQ-015 to enable operator review of all remote actions; denied attempts must be logged (not only successful executions) so that policy violations are visible without executing the denied command.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary SHALL terminate an SSH subprocess and return an error result after 30 s of execution for bounded commands (service status, log tail, restart, health check), and SHALL use an async streaming execution path for long-running commands (deploy, backup) with no fixed ceiling, streaming output as produced via IFC-RAG-026 and terminating only on remote process exit or explicit operator cancel; concurrent active SSH sessions SHALL be limited to 4 at all times.
items.rationale: STK-REQ-009 and IFC-REQ-004 define an allowlisted command set that explicitly includes deploy and backup, which routinely exceed 30 s. A single 30-second hard ceiling would error every deploy and backup action, defeating two of the six allowlisted remote actions. The command class split (bounded 30 s vs async streaming) resolves the contradiction with IFC-RAG-026, which anticipates streaming output for long-running remote sessions. The 4-session cap prevents resource exhaustion under concurrent adapter bursts.

items.text: The Remote SSH Boundary SHALL execute each remote SSH command using the host operator's existing SSH configuration and SHALL pass the command text through the active allowlist before spawning the SSH subprocess.
items.rationale: Derived from SYS-REQ-015; the allowlist pre-check before subprocess spawn prevents any command not listed in the allowlist from ever reaching the remote host, which is the system-level security guarantee.
